# Cursor Documentation - Content Extraction

## Core Product Information
**Cursor** is described as "an AI editor and coding agent" built to make users "extraordinarily productive." The platform enables users to understand codebases, plan and build features, fix bugs, review changes, and integrate with existing tools.

## Key Features & Capabilities
- Understanding unfamiliar code
- Creating and building features
- Finding and fixing bugs
- Code review functionality
- Debugging assistance
- Test generation
- Documentation generation
- Code modernization and refactoring
- Rapid prototyping

## Supported Languages & Frameworks
The documentation references support for:
- JavaScript & TypeScript
- Python
- Java
- C#
- iOS & macOS (Swift)
- Data Science workflows (Python, R, SQL)
- Web development

## AI Models Available
Multiple Claude models are documented:
- Claude 4.5 Haiku
- Claude 4.5 Sonnet
- Claude 4.6 Sonnet
- Claude Opus variants (4.5, 4.6, 4.6 Fast)

Additional models from other providers:
- Google Gemini series (2.5 Flash, 3.x variants)
- OpenAI GPT-5 series (multiple versions)
- Grok-4-20, Kimi-k2-5

## Cursor-Specific Models
- Cursor Composer 1, 1.5, and 2

## Enterprise & Team Features
- Admin API and analytics
- Team dashboards and member management
- SSO capabilities
- SCIM integration
- Service accounts
- Billing groups
- Cloud Agent deployment (self-hosted and Kubernetes options)
- Private workers
- Compliance monitoring

## Developer Tools
- CLI with authentication, configuration, and shell mode
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration
- Browser tools
- Terminal access
- GitHub and GitLab integrations
- Linear integration
- Slack integration
- JetBrains integration

## Additional Services
- Bugbot for automated code review
- Cloud Agent with automations
- Skills and subagents
- Rules engine
- Plugins system
- Hooks for third-party integration
- Webhooks for Cloud Agent API

## Documentation Structure
The site includes sections for Get Started, Models & Pricing, Changelog, and comprehensive guides for various user roles (designers, product managers, sales engineers, support teams).
